description_long: This object is a classic corded telephone with a rectangular base unit and an extended, straight handset resting on top. The base features a small display and a keypad area, likely for dialing numbers, with a few visible buttons and indicator lights. A long, straight cord extends from the handset to the base, suggesting a wired connection for audio transmission. The front of the base has a slot or speaker grille, and the rear shows multiple ports or connectors, possibly for power and telephone line input. The overall design is utilitarian, with a matte black finish and minimal decorative elements, suitable for practical use. The object appears robust and is likely constructed from hard plastic with some internal metal components.
description: A matte black corded telephone with a rectangular base, visible keypad, and a long, straight handset connected by a cord. The design is utilitarian and robust, with indicator lights and multiple ports.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the handset and cord, with the base's profile and a small display visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the keypad area, indicator lights, and the end of the handset resting on the cradle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, showing the handset and cord extending from the base.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying multiple ports or connectors and the back of the handset, with the cord extending outward.
